heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 225 on 28 May 2026, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 1st among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 22 days so far.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwave.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (208°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 7.1°C) and outside temperature (down 5.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 12%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 1312 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1976 m) and outside humidity (down 13%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1312.4m lower than yesterday, 1975.6m lower than the past week, 1522.4m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 1490m and a high of 5530m, averaging 2280m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and the light is absent. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; there will not be a great deal of drying overnight.
